name: weave
description: A design-to-storefront studio · hand-built designs become verified, deployed websites.

stages.title: Part 1 · Finish the foundation honestly (“W2R”)
stages.note: One item at a time · ends at the phase-2 milestone

phases.name: Tidy the workshop
phases.desc: Clean-up so everything after starts from a known-good state.
items: Old leftover work branches removed and all history published online.
The plans locked in as the single source of truth.
A tamper-check list (canon.hashes) so key documents can't drift silently.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: Missing screens get drawn
phases.desc: The designer (Claude Design) is drawing 3 missing screens + 29 touch-ups.
items: A “publish is held” screen · shown when quality checks block a launch.
A “fix your fonts” screen · for designs using fonts the build can't load.
Website-capture screens (blocked / in progress / failed).
29 smaller corrections across existing screens.

paste.when: Now · this is what's in design
When the batch returns · the session runs the acceptance

phases.name: The beauty pass
phases.desc: 17 polish edits · run it the moment R-1 comes back, in the same sitting.
items: Fix light mode (titles are nearly invisible on white today).
Design the charts for real (placeholders today).
One clear main button per screen.
Dim finished-item thumbnails so your eye lands on what needs attention.
Dozens of small alignment, wording, and consistency fixes.

paste.when: The moment R-1 comes back · same sitting, then one acceptance covers both

phases.name: Fresh-eyes screen review
phases.desc: An independent reviewer compares every built screen to its drawing, once · with the beauty pass already applied, so each screen is judged exactly one time.
items: Each of the 16 built screens is compared to its drawing · layout, spacing, type, colors, every state · in dark AND light mode.
The reviewer is independent and never saw how the screens were built; “looks right” is not a pass.
Every difference becomes a written fix item; this is also how the beauty pass lands in the app.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: No check can be skipped
phases.desc: Closes the loophole where a broken or missing quality check could quietly count as a pass.
items: Today a broken or missing quality check can slip through as a pass. After this, anything missing blocks the build and says why.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: Stricter visual checking
phases.desc: The design-vs-build comparison gets teeth: more properties, a phone-width pass, honest coverage numbers, and fair treatment of app widgets.
items: A pinned list of visual properties compared design-vs-build: colors, sizes, spacing, whether fonts actually loaded.
A phone-width (390px) check for every section.
App-widget areas judged fairly · layout checked, the app's own content honestly marked “not ours to judge”.
A coverage rule: if important parts couldn't be checked, the result says so instead of quietly passing.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: Design intake gets real
phases.desc: Everything that enters a build · designs, images, fonts · becomes verified, real input before any money is spent generating.
items: Live Figma import · always the exact version you pasted.
Every image genuinely uploaded to the store (no grey placeholders).
Fonts verified to load · or you're asked to fix them before money is spent.
Bad input refused up front with a clear reason.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: Publish & undo become real
phases.desc: The launch path stops pretending: real publish, a real hold when checks fail, real undo · and the live result re-checked after upload.
items: The publish button really publishes.
A failing check holds the launch, blockers listed.
Undo genuinely restores and says what's live now.
Your approval is tied to the exact build you approved.
After upload, the live preview is re-checked · passing locally isn't enough.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: No dead ends
phases.desc: Every journey completes: create, cancel, go back and restart all work, and no button ever silently does nothing.
items: Create, cancel, go back, restart all work everywhere.
Every button does something or says why it can't.
Loading and error states on every screen.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: A steadier engine
phases.desc: The machinery underneath gets dependable: polite retries on hiccups, one repair loop, and no silent guessing on incomplete AI answers.
items: The AI connection retries politely and never pretends success.
One repair loop instead of two competing ones.
No silent guessing on incomplete AI answers · gaps are named.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

phases.name: Sign off & stamp the milestone
phases.desc: The closing ceremony: the full manual test book runs end to end, every known problem is settled on paper, and the phase-2 milestone is stamped.
items: Run the full book of manual tests, as a non-coder would.
Settle every known problem: fixed, or moved with a written reason.
Stamp the phase-2 milestone.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session just for this item

stages.title: Part 2 · Speed & sturdiness
stages.note: Background work · rolls alongside the next phases

phases.name: Faster and harder to break
phases.desc: A bundle of improvements slotted in as capacity allows.
items: Interrupted builds resume · no paying twice.
Several sections build at once.
Smart caching cuts the AI bill.
Good past sections get reused.
A “plant one bug, prove the right check catches it” suite for the checker itself.

phases.name: Table stakes
phases.desc: The everyday-app essentials.
items: A home dashboard with real numbers and charts.
Build history with one-click undo.
Rename & delete projects safely.
Translation tools (+ a checker for a classic translation mistake).
Quick search (⌘K).
Launch checks · the app says up front if your Mac is missing something.
A live preview panel.
A “save a problem report” button.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Talk to your build
phases.desc: Ask for changes in plain English.
items: A chat that makes real changes · every one still passes the same checks.
A failed check inside chat offers retry, or opens the full board.
Protected areas (a client's own edits) refuse changes and say why.
An “accept with a note” path exists · but it's always visible at publish, never a quiet pass.
Spending limits · chat pauses and asks, never runs up a bill.
If the store changed while you chatted, it stops and asks before overwriting anything.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Second output: standalone site
phases.desc: Not everything is a store · Weave learns to build standalone websites with a content editor behind them, held to the same checks.
items: A standalone website output with a friendly content editor (CMS) behind it.
The same visual checks apply to it · plus a code check that blocks the build on any break.
One project can produce both outputs, with honest side-by-side statuses.
Publishing the site's content to production gets its own confirm / failure / held screens.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Explore looks
phases.desc: A playground for finding the right look · generate directions, tune the feel · safely separated from anything that can ship.
items: Generate several design directions and compare them side by side.
A “feel” mode: tune colors, type, and mood with simple controls.
Everything here is clearly marked unverified · nothing can deploy from Explore.
Promote the winner into a real build that goes through every check.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

paste.label: the light-mode design batch
paste.when: Written at W3b's close · doesn't exist yet

phases.name: Quick mockups
phases.desc: Rough ideas, fast: throwaway prototypes and remixes with no path to deploy · for thinking, not shipping.
items: Fast throwaway prototypes for trying ideas.
Remix an existing design into variations.
Clearly separated from the verified pipeline · no deploy path at all.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Start from a live website
phases.desc: Weave learns to start from the real world · capture an existing site as a starting point · and to update itself safely.
items: Point Weave at a live website and capture it as a starting point.
A safety guard refuses private/internal addresses · visibly, with the reason.
Scripted capture steps (dismiss cookie banners, open menus) that fail loudly if they can't run.
The app can update itself · snapshot first, and it refuses to open data from a newer version.
Optional add-on packs installed from Settings, with honest failure and cleanup.
A merchant's own edits stay protected during rebuilds.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: The right AI for each job
phases.desc: The engine gets taste and memory: work routed to the best model for it, and learning loops that advise but can never approve.
items: Each job goes to the AI model best (and cheapest) for it, with a visible scoreboard.
Remembered repairs: fixes that worked before get offered again.
Early warning for sections that historically never converge · parked early, honestly.
Real usage data feeds tips into the user guide.
Suggested tolerance tweaks arrive with evidence and wait for YOUR approval.
Learning only advises · it can never approve or ship anything.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Code a Shopify expert would sign
phases.desc: The output stops at merely-correct and reaches craftsmanship · code that reads like a senior Shopify developer wrote it by hand.
items: Output that reads like hand-crafted modern Shopify work · editable blocks, theme colors, clean layout.
Plain-English labels a store owner understands.
Proper mounting points so app widgets have somewhere official to live.
A store owner can rearrange things in Shopify's own editor without breaking the design.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

phases.name: Whole sites, wired together
phases.desc: From sections to sites: pages, menus and links inferred from the design, confirmed by you · and no dead link can ever ship.
items: Multi-page builds: which page is which (home, product, collection…) is inferred from the design.
Menus and links are proposed, then YOU confirm · edit, stub, or drop each unresolved link.
Store data like navigation menus is created through the official channels, never invented.
The hard rule: no dead link can ever ship.

paste.when: A fresh terminal session for this phase

paste.label: its design batch
paste.when: Created at this phase's start · doesn't exist yet

phases.name: Capture → redesign → ship
phases.desc: The full loop: take something real from the web, redesign it anywhere from a touch-up to a re-skin, and ship it verified.
items: Redesign a captured site anywhere from touch-up to re-skin.
One approval gate for the capture; after that the original site is never consulted again.
Ships to either output, fully checked.

paste.when: This phase has its OWN prompt · not the canon one
When this phase starts

phases.name: Setup & the built-in guide
phases.desc: The product learns to explain itself: a setup checker that names exactly what's missing, and a guide anyone can read.
items: A setup checker with seven live rows (AI key, Figma, Shopify, browsers, network, disk…) naming exactly what's missing and the fix.
An 18-chapter built-in user guide in plain language · no internals, ever.
Two independent reviews keep the guide honest: one hunts leaked jargon, one walks every step against the real app.
Possible extra: an exportable “what was built and checked” report for clients.

paste.when: This phase has its OWN prompt · not the canon one
After the phase's opening decisions

phases.name: Real app integrations
phases.desc: Reviews from the review app · not drawn stars.
items: A plugin system that teaches Weave real Shopify apps · data only; no app code ever runs inside Weave.
Bundled knowledge for the 10 most-used apps (reviews, email, subscriptions, search…), each fact sourced from the app's official docs.
Automatic detection of which apps a connected store already uses.
What an app teaches can make builds smarter and checks stricter · never looser; the judge never reads it.
Two new Settings screens: Integrations and per-project Rules.
Per-client rules you write once (“this client's header never moves”) that every build respects.
Start builds from your own custom base theme, snapshotted safely at project creation.
App keys stay on your Mac, masked everywhere, never visible to merchants.
Five new manual tests close the phase.

paste.when: This phase has its OWN prompt · not the canon one
After the phase's opening decisions

phases.name: Custom storefront on Shopify
phases.desc: A fully custom site on Shopify's checkout & catalog.
items: A third output: the storefront is fully custom; the merchant keeps working in Shopify as usual.
The builder outputs the same sections, wired to live products from the store.
Checks stay repeatable by testing against a snapshot of the catalog taken at each run's start.
See it working on sample products before entering any keys.
Real deploys: staging preview first, then production with the destination spelled out · undo kept.
Existing projects can add this output · same design, no rework; a failure on one output never touches another.
Version pins so Shopify's quarterly updates can't quietly break things.
New deploy-state screens + five new manual tests.

paste.when: This phase has its OWN prompt · not the canon one
After the phase's opening decisions

phases.name: Ready to sell
phases.desc: The final phase: everything needed to hand Weave to a stranger · sign-in, subscription, spending caps, security testing, and the legal basics.
items: A small online service (the “broker”) holds the AI keys · the app itself never ships a secret.
Subscriptions through a payment provider, with billing events handled safely.
Sign in with Google from the app · the browser does the login; nothing sensitive touches the app.
Your AI usage flows through the subscription with a live usage meter.
Running out pauses honestly with clear options (raise the cap, upgrade) · work is never lost.
A hard spending cap that YOU set; overage is opt-in only.
An account screen: plan, usage bar, devices, billing, sign-out, delete-account.
A sign-in gate at launch, honest offline behavior (a one-week lease).
A security specialist attacks the whole flow before ship; every finding fixed or refuted on record.
Terms, privacy, and a named support channel.
Final walkthroughs on the real packaged app · including one real purchase and refund.

paste.when: This phase has its OWN prompt · not the canon one
After the phase's opening decisions

phases.name: Shopper-facing superpowers
phases.desc: 20 candidate features for the sites Weave builds.
items: Virtual try-on, honest urgency counters, a product Q&A that only says what it can prove, AI-shopper readiness, instant search, and 15 more.

phases.name: Agency monitoring service
phases.desc: A possible future cloud product. Your call (OD-3).
items: Continuously watch client stores for breakage and prove changes are safe · parked with its own go/no-go checklist.

actions.text: Remove the leftover “medan” workspace · the last clean-up loose end.
actions.cmd: git worktree remove --force ~/conductor/workspaces/weave/medan && git -C ~/Documents/weave branch -d medan

actions.text: R-1 is in design · run the drawing batch in Claude Design; when it's back, run the beauty pass (EL-1) in the same sitting, then accept both together.
Run the beauty pass (EL-1) now · before R-2 starts, so every screen is only reviewed once.
Three decisions wait on you (OD-1 roadmap scope · OD-2 paid pilots · OD-3 monitoring service) · due at the next sign-off.
